programme.title: Rhod Gilbert: The Book of John
programme.desc: A stand-up performance by the comedian filmed live at the Wales Millennium Centre in Cardiff, which was his first live show in six years. Raw, personal and brutally honest like never before, Rhod takes us on a no-nonsense journey into finding humour when youve hit rock bottom - and the unexpected encounter that leads you to a lower place

programme.title: Bill Bailey: Cosmic Jam
programme.desc: In his classic debut live stand-up show, filmed at Londons Bloomsbury Theatre in 1996, Bill explains why Bryan Adams is perhaps the greatest ever exponent of the rock anthem; demonstrates the influence that Cockney music has had on the great classical composers; and warns of the perils of a world ruled by insects, while playing the second-biggest gong in Britain

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Long Island, New York, to help an Indian restaurant which used to run a Mexican eatery out of the same kitchen. Its been operating for 35-years, but a decline in business led to fusion experiments, which havent always been successful and which have confused the customers. With some dishes tasting soggy and swimming with oil, Ramsay has his work cut out trying to turn things round

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Another selection of BandB owners take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. The contest begins at Knockaloe Beg Farm on the Isle of Man, where owners Fiona and John Anderson hope their guests will embrace their green ethos. They kick things off with an afternoon of laser mayhem, and continue the fun the next day by following breakfast with hands-on animal antics

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests head to the Arches on the Isle of Man for their second stay, where owner Phil Routledge hopes to wow them with scenic views and scuba diving at the BandBs own pool. However, some of the owners are dismayed by modern gadgets with a lack of instructions, and even find cobwebs in their rooms

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants arrive at the Trafford in Blackpool, owned by Robbie and John Welling, who are proud of their traditional bucket and spade BandB. The guests are pleasantly surprised, even if previous host Phil is intent on finding dust as a bit of tit-for-tat retaliation, and the afternoon is spent learning the art of making sticks of rock

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Host Julie welcomes the contestants to Sheepy Lodge, Warwickshire, where they are shocked by their surroundings, and disappointed by a variety of things in the rooms. The host puts on some old-fashioned parlour games, before a fierce hide-and-seek contest erupts. At breakfast, Julie frets when she must prepare poached eggs, and is left emotional after the feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rival BandB owners meet to settle some scores and discover what they have been paid. Knockaloe Beg Farm owners Fiona and John Anderson tackle criticisms of a missing host at breakfast, and Phil Routledge from The Arches on the Isle of Man takes on Robbie and John Welling from The Trafford in Blackpool about their forensic investigation. After some heated discussion, the winner is announced

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Highlights of the food and chat show, with Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: With the chapel being unliveable for many more months, Keith and Marj have come up with a radical idea to sleep onsite a lot sooner, in the shape of a shipping container in their car park, which they hope to transform into their first official finished bedroom

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug lies to Carrie about working late so he can get out of a seminar and play mud football, but his escapade in the rain makes him so feverish that he has a nightmare about appearing in TV shows with Arthur. Comedy, starring Kevin James

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Carrie wants Arthur to socialise more and encourages her father to attend an old peoples centre, which he hates. Doug takes pity on his father-in-law and takes him to a cocktail party instead - but ends up having to take sole responsibility for him. Kevin James stars

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug feels unappreciated at work and aims to get a pay rise by claiming a major courier service wants to hire him - but he ends up getting sacked. Meanwhile, Carries enthusiasm for tanning lotion proves disastrous. With Lou Ferrigno, star of cult TV series The Incredible Hulk

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Marge compete to be the subject of Lisas Most Interesting Person I Know essay, but instead she chooses Professor Frink. With the guest voice of Jim Parsons

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Todd, Todd, Why Hast Thou Forsaken Me?
programme.desc: Todd Flanders stops believing in God so Ned asks the Simpsons to look after his son for a while and help him rediscover his faith

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Ed Jackson and Billy Monger present coverage of the opening events on the first day of the Games in Italy. The men and womens para alpine downhill at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre sees Neil Simpson defend his title after becoming the first British male to win gold on the snow alongside his guide, and brother, Andrew in the super-G in Beijing. Meanwhile, the wheelchair curling mixed team event begins with Great Britain taking on Slovakia at Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Continued coverage of the opening day of the Games, held in Milan and Cortina dAmpezzo, Italy. Great Britains Scott Meenagh competes in his third and final Paralympics in the para biathlon 7.5km sprint finals sitting at Tesero Cross-Country Skiing Stadium. In 2025, he won bronze in the Para Biathlon Sprint World Cup in Sweden, so now hes looking to keep up the momentum and earn another medal as a final farewell to a spectacular career. His compatriot and three-time world champion Menna Fitzpatrick, with her guide Katie Guest, is also bidding for glory in the alpine skiing downhill final at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre. With reports from Dame Sarah Storey and Alice Tai

programme.title: Formula 1
programme.desc: Steve Jones presents action from the battle for grid positions in the opening round of the season, held at Albert Park Circuit in Melbourne. Lando Norris took pole position here last year and his McLaren teammate Oscar Piastri joined him on the front row. With analysis from David Coulthard and Mark Webber

programme.title: Crufts 2026
programme.desc: Radzi Chinyanganya and Clare Balding present all the action with live coverage from the NEC in Birmingham. Claudia Winkleman is roving the halls to cover all the hot topics in the dog world. Meanwhile, Ashleigh Butler joins Jim Rosenthal and Graham Partridge in the commentary box to add insight to the skills on show in the Large Agility competition

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Highlights
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan presents action from day one of the Games, held in Milan and Cortina dAmpezzo, Italy. Paralympic GBs Menna Fitzpatrick and Neil Simpson both went for gold in the downhill alpine skiing at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre. Plus, Alice Tai reports from Tesero Cross-Country Skiing Stadium, where Scott Meenagh, Sam Cozens and Fred Warburton competed for Great Britain in the super-G, and Dame Sarah Storey provides curling updates from Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium

programme.title: Crufts 2026
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman joins Clare Balding on a star-studded Saturday night at the worlds greatest dog show, in Birmingham. The road to Best in Show continues with judging of both the Utility Group, including schnauzers and poodles, and the pocket rockets of the canine world - the Toy dogs. Ellie Simmonds talks about her Dog of the Day, while Scruffts gives crossbred dogs their chance to shine in categories including Most Handsome, Golden Oldie, and the Good Citizen Dog Scheme. And it wouldnt be Saturday evening without Claudia Winkleman witnessing some top-class dancing. Its not strictly ballroom, but the Heelwork to Music dogs and owners will be strutting their stuff in the International Freestyle competition

programme.title: Bill Baileys Vietnam
programme.desc: Bill travels down the Indochina peninsula, exploring the profound changes of the past half-century. Arriving in Ho Chi Minh City, the most populous city in Vietnam, Bill finds himself caught between wartime tunnels, endless traffic, and the music genres of metal and folk. With the expert assistance of scooter guide KK, Bill wonders if he can find old Saigon in modern Ho Chi Minh City

programme.title: The Unlikely Pilgrimage of Harold Fry (2023)
programme.desc: Devon-based pensioner Harold Fry receives a letter from his dying former work colleague, Queenie. She is living out her final few days in a Northumberland hospice. Harold spontaneously embarks on a physically gruelling 500-mile trek north. Drama based on Rachel Joyces novel, starring Jim Broadbent and Penelope Wilton

programme.title: Fury (2014)
programme.desc: A veteran American sergeant leads a tank crew on one final dangerous mission in the last months of the Second World War. The assignment takes the soldiers behind enemy lines and into battle with overwhelming enemy forces, while the presence of an inexperienced new recruit in the team raises tension. Drama, starring Brad Pitt and Shia LaBeouf

programme.title: Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing
programme.desc: Jonathan Ross hosts this reality challenge in which nine pairs of strangers with different beliefs, lifestyles, opinions and habits are handcuffed together for as long as they can stand it. The pairs must navigate eating, dressing and even sleeping just inches apart as they are sent on a road trip across the UK - with the ones who last the longest in the running for £100,000. From a bar tender and a millionaire to an alpha male and a proud feminist, which couple will be the first to take the cuffs off?

programme.title: Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing
programme.desc: The unlikely manacled couples are still living cheek by jowl as they get to know each other - for better or for worse. Housewife and self-confessed prude Charlie hopes being handcuffed to a total stranger will help broaden her mind. After her initial shock at being chained to 6ft 7 heavily tattooed Rob, they settle into domestic bliss at Charlies cottage. Meanwhile, the forced friendship between Christian hairdresser Nina and chatty mum of seven Sara could be the start of something beautiful - until the former realises just how talkative her partner is

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer catch up with Rupreet and Sundeep who, in 2011, reached out for their help searching for their first home which had to be in a particular area of Harrow and near by to their own families. In 2021, Laura and Euan moved from Sydney back home to Glasgow so their children could be closer to their grandparents but after so long away they were torn between buying a flat in the trendy West End or moving a bit further out to get a larger family home. Kirstie and Phil catch up with the pair to see where they are now

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A particularly bad episode of Itchy and Scratchy prompts Bart and Lisa to write their own script and send it to the shows producer under Grampas name. With the voice of Brooke Shields

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart grows frustrated by Homers lack of interest in him and sets out to search for a new father figure. Lisa tries to break her addiction to an expensive teenage chat-line, but finds it harder than expected

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: During the Palmers separation, Doug and Carrie quarrel about allowing Kelly to go on a date. Comedy, starring Kevin James and Leah Remini

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug meets his parents at the airport where they urge him to sign their will - which includes a clause stating that he is responsible for turning off their life support. He is horrified at the thought, but Stephanie is just jealous that her brother is the favourite yet again

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa gets more than she bargained for from her new braces, while things get awkward when Artie Ziffs bride-to-be proves to look just like Marge

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Springfield steals the Sea Captains treasure to build a science, technology, engineering and mathematics academy. However, Lisa and Bart discover that the school has terrifying hidden plans

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge becomes obsessed with getting the perfect vacation photo when the Simpson family are finally invited on the Van Houtens annual trip to Costa Rica

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Ed Jackson and Billy Monger present coverage of the opening events on day two of the Games from Italy. The men and womens snowboard cross finals begin at Cortina Para Snowboard Park, and James Barnes-Miller, the first ever snowboarder to represent ParalympicsGB, will be looking to take home a medal in his third Games. Elsewhere, Scott Meenagh competes in the 12.5km individual sitting final in para biathlon at Tesero Cross-Country Skiing Stadium, and the British wheelchair mixed team curling team face Canada at Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium. With reports from Dame Sarah Storey and Alice Tai

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Ed Jackson and Billy Monger present further live coverage of day two of the Games, as more medals are contested in Italy. The snowboard cross continues at Cortina Para Snowboard Park, with former motocross rider Ollie Hill and Steamboat World Cup bronze medallist Nina Sparks among the British contenders

programme.title: Formula 1
programme.desc: Steve Jones presents action from the opening race of the season, held at Albert Park Circuit in Melbourne. Lando Norris of McLaren was the victorious driver here last year, with Red Bulls Max Verstappen finishing second and George Russell of Mercedes taking third. That round proved very much to be a sign of things to come, with Norris going on to beat Verstappen to the title and Russell finishing fourth in the standings behind the champions teammate Oscar Piastri. With analysis from David Coulthard, Mark Webber and Lawrence Barretto

programme.title: Crufts 2026
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman joins our host Clare Balding at the National Exhibition Centre in Birmingham on the final day of action at the worlds greatest dog show. This afternoons programme features the semi-finals and final of the Flyball competition, which is wide open because the reigning champions are absent. The Intermediate and Large dogs tackle the obstacles on the Agility course, as excitement builds towards the climax of Crufts 2026

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Highlights
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan presents action from day two of the Games, held in Italy. The snowboard cross finals took centre-stage at Cortina Para Snowboard Park, with a number of British hopefuls involved. Plus, the round-robin stage continued in the wheelchair curling at Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium

programme.title: Crufts 2026
programme.desc: Clare Balding and Claudia Winkleman introduce the grand finale of this years dog show, as the Best in Show winner is declared. Plus, the judging of the Gundog Group, the Hero Dogs of 2026, and the presenting team select the breeds that look most like them

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: Siobhán McSweeney and judges Keith Brymer Jones and Rich Miller present the final, where the three remaining potters create miniature model theatres and take on a tricky throwing technique in their last surprise second challenge. Whose final curtain call will crown them as champion?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on The Apprentice, Britains Got Talent, Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing, Love Is Blind, Celebrity Puzzling, Alexander Armstrong in India and ITV News about the conflict in Iran

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Dating show in which singletons meet up with possible partners at a restaurant in the hope of finding love, before deciding whether they want to take things further. Newly married Fred Sirieix watches on as serial dater Conner, from Surrey, tries to connect with tech consultant Charlie, a self-described geek who turns heads. Next in is Sophie, a tattoo-loving mum-of-three from Cheshire who has never dated a woman before. Her date, Emily, is a massage therapist with a calming energy. The two bond quickly, but a twist lies in store as it becomes apparent that one has her husband in tow

programme.title: Freaky (2020)
programme.desc: Seventeen-year-old Millie Kessler spends her days trying to survive high school and the cruel actions of the popular crowd. But when she becomes the latest target of the Butcher, the towns infamous serial killer, her senior year becomes the least of her worries. When the Butchers mystical dagger causes him and Millie to magically switch bodies, the frightened teen learns she has just 24 hours to get her identity back before she looks like a middle-aged maniac for ever. Comedy horror, starring Vince Vaughn and Kathryn Newton

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to New Jerseys Spanish Pavillion, which is run by two warring brothers whose staff spend more time using their mobile phones than working. The chef plans to turn the eaterys fortunes around, and starts by giving the dining room and menu a modern makeover

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Three spooky tales for the shows 30th Halloween special, including a parody of Stranger Things, and a dead Homers spirit trying on some new bodies for size

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa schemes to get her own back on Bart for destroying the prize tomato she grew for a science project. Meanwhile, Homer is arrested for drink-driving and finds himself having to attend Alcoholics Anonymous meetings

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa tires of being followed everywhere by a lovestruck Ralph and hurts his feelings by blurting out in public that she cannot stand him - prompting disgruntled local lawman Chief Wiggum to harass Homer in retaliation

programme.title: Jamie Cooks Spring
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver goes through the year, celebrating seasonal ingredients and what can be made with them as they come into season in the UK. He goes on a delicious journey through the kitchen calendar, highlighting weird, wonderful and wonky produce with inspiring recipes for when theyre at their best. He begins by picking some rhubarb from the garden and making a lip-smacking sauce for marinated pork belly, pan-fried until crispy in his hot and sour rhubarb crispy pork noodles, a special dish for any springtime occasion. He picks some new season spinach for a luxurious spinach and goats cheese risotto, before cooking up a scruffy spring tart using leeks. Jamie also demonstrates how to divide shop-bought basil into lots of individual plants to last through the year and celebrates the vibrantly coloured stalks and leaves of chard in a crowd-pleasing cheesy spring cannelloni

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp is enlisted to help identical twins Matt and Chris Wickham buy their first home in north London, but a budget of £350,000 may not be enough to secure a property in their favoured areas of Pimlico and Victoria. Meanwhile, Phil Spencer offers advice to Sam Taylor and his fiancee Krystle Maher, who are keen to move from their rented flat in Deptford into a place of their own

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with comedian Neil Delamere joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: A performance by childrens entertainer Nanny Gee in honour of Fredericks second birthday helps to shed some light on Frasiers romantic past, but leaves Lilith in a jealous rage. Comedy, guest starring Emma Thompson

programme.title: Cheers
programme.sub-title: A Diminished Rebecca with a Suspended Cliff
programme.desc: Rebecca claims to Sam that she can run the bar for a night just as efficiently as he, but has to reassess her boast when she discovers that she has forgotten to renew the liquor licence. Meanwhile, Woody adopts the role of cardsharp when a scorching game of poker develops in the poolroom

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Rebecca is left in charge of the bar while the others play poker but soon realises shes out of her depth when she discovers the liquor licence has expired. Bar-room comedy, starring Kirstie Alley

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Ed Jackson and Billy Monger present coverage of the opening events on day three of the Games in Italy. Menna Fitzpatrick, Hester Poole, Sam Cozens, Fred Warburton and Neil Simpson are among the British hopefuls in the super-G finals at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre. Plus, Great Britain take on South Korea in the round robin stage of the mixed team wheelchair curling at Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Ed Jackson and Billy Monger present coverage of the opening events on day three of the Games, as the super-G finals and South Korea v Great Britain in the round robin stage of the mixed team curling both continue at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre and Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium respectively

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Midday news summary

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Further live coverage of day three of the Games, including Italy v Great Britain in the round robin stage of the wheelchair curling mixed doubles at Cortina Olympic Curling Stadium. The British pairing of Jason Kean and Jo Butterfield are both making their Winter Paralympic debut, but Kean was part of the England team that qualified for the Wheelchair Curling World Championship in 2025, and Butterfield won a gold at the Summer Paralympics in the club throw back in 2016 in Rio

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Colin Jackson in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against words and numbers

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Jen and Nick from Essex set up a new charity for retired sports horses in France, while Brian and Paula from Devon fight back against the odds to give French life a second shot. Driven by his dream of living by the beach, coffee bar owner James brings his British business idea to Spain

programme.title: Bill Bailey: Cosmic Jam
programme.desc: In his classic debut live stand-up show, filmed at Londons Bloomsbury Theatre in 1996, Bill explains why Bryan Adams is perhaps the greatest ever exponent of the rock anthem; demonstrates the influence that Cockney music has had on the great classical composers; and warns of the perils of a world ruled by insects, while playing the second-biggest gong in Britain

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Long Island, New York, to help an Indian restaurant which used to run a Mexican eatery out of the same kitchen. Its been operating for 35-years, but a decline in business led to fusion experiments, which havent always been successful and which have confused the customers. With some dishes tasting soggy and swimming with oil, Ramsay has his work cut out trying to turn things round

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Another selection of BandB owners take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. The contest begins at Knockaloe Beg Farm on the Isle of Man, where owners Fiona and John Anderson hope their guests will embrace their green ethos. They kick things off with an afternoon of laser mayhem, and continue the fun the next day by following breakfast with hands-on animal antics

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests head to the Arches on the Isle of Man for their second stay, where owner Phil Routledge hopes to wow them with scenic views and scuba diving at the BandBs own pool. However, some of the owners are dismayed by modern gadgets with a lack of instructions, and even find cobwebs in their rooms

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants arrive at the Trafford in Blackpool, owned by Robbie and John Welling, who are proud of their traditional bucket and spade BandB. The guests are pleasantly surprised, even if previous host Phil is intent on finding dust as a bit of tit-for-tat retaliation, and the afternoon is spent learning the art of making sticks of rock

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Host Julie welcomes the contestants to Sheepy Lodge, Warwickshire, where they are shocked by their surroundings, and disappointed by a variety of things in the rooms. The host puts on some old-fashioned parlour games, before a fierce hide-and-seek contest erupts. At breakfast, Julie frets when she must prepare poached eggs, and is left emotional after the feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rival BandB owners meet to settle some scores and discover what they have been paid. Knockaloe Beg Farm owners Fiona and John Anderson tackle criticisms of a missing host at breakfast, and Phil Routledge from The Arches on the Isle of Man takes on Robbie and John Welling from The Trafford in Blackpool about their forensic investigation. After some heated discussion, the winner is announced

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Highlights of the food and chat show, with Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: With the chapel being unliveable for many more months, Keith and Marj have come up with a radical idea to sleep onsite a lot sooner, in the shape of a shipping container in their car park, which they hope to transform into their first official finished bedroom

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug lies to Carrie about working late so he can get out of a seminar and play mud football, but his escapade in the rain makes him so feverish that he has a nightmare about appearing in TV shows with Arthur. Comedy, starring Kevin James

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Carrie wants Arthur to socialise more and encourages her father to attend an old peoples centre, which he hates. Doug takes pity on his father-in-law and takes him to a cocktail party instead - but ends up having to take sole responsibility for him. Kevin James stars

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug feels unappreciated at work and aims to get a pay rise by claiming a major courier service wants to hire him - but he ends up getting sacked. Meanwhile, Carries enthusiasm for tanning lotion proves disastrous. With Lou Ferrigno, star of cult TV series The Incredible Hulk

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Marge compete to be the subject of Lisas Most Interesting Person I Know essay, but instead she chooses Professor Frink. With the guest voice of Jim Parsons

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Todd, Todd, Why Hast Thou Forsaken Me?
programme.desc: Todd Flanders stops believing in God so Ned asks the Simpsons to look after his son for a while and help him rediscover his faith

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Ed Jackson and Billy Monger present coverage of the opening events on the first day of the Games in Italy. programme.desc: Siobhán McSweeney and judges Keith Brymer Jones and Rich Miller present the final, where the three remaining potters create miniature model theatres and take on a tricky throwing technique in their last surprise second challenge. Whose final curtain call will crown them as champion?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on The Apprentice, Britains Got Talent, Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing, Love Is Blind, Celebrity Puzzling, Alexander Armstrong in India and ITV News about the conflict in Iran

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Dating show in which singletons meet up with possible partners at a restaurant in the hope of finding love, before deciding whether they want to take things further. Newly married Fred Sirieix watches on as serial dater Conner, from Surrey, tries to connect with tech consultant Charlie, a self-described geek who turns heads. Next in is Sophie, a tattoo-loving mum-of-three from Cheshire who has never dated a woman before. Her date, Emily, is a massage therapist with a calming energy. The two bond quickly, but a twist lies in store as it becomes apparent that one has her husband in tow

programme.title: Freaky (2020)
programme.desc: Seventeen-year-old Millie Kessler spends her days trying to survive high school and the cruel actions of the popular crowd. But when she becomes the latest target of the Butcher, the towns infamous serial killer, her senior year becomes the least of her worries. When the Butchers mystical dagger causes him and Millie to magically switch bodies, the frightened teen learns she has just 24 hours to get her identity back before she looks like a middle-aged maniac for ever. Comedy horror, starring Vince Vaughn and Kathryn Newton

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to New Jerseys Spanish Pavillion, which is run by two warring brothers whose staff spend more time using their mobile phones than working. The chef plans to turn the eaterys fortunes around, and starts by giving the dining room and menu a modern makeover

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Three spooky tales for the shows 30th Halloween special, including a parody of Stranger Things, and a dead Homers spirit trying on some new bodies for size

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa schemes to get her own back on Bart for destroying the prize tomato she grew for a science project. Meanwhile, Homer is arrested for drink-driving and finds himself having to attend Alcoholics Anonymous meetings

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa tires of being followed everywhere by a lovestruck Ralph and hurts his feelings by blurting out in public that she cannot stand him - prompting disgruntled local lawman Chief Wiggum to harass Homer in retaliation

programme.title: Jamie Cooks Spring
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver goes through the year, celebrating seasonal ingredients and what can be made with them as they come into season in the UK. He goes on a delicious journey through the kitchen calendar, highlighting weird, wonderful and wonky produce with inspiring recipes for when theyre at their best. He begins by picking some rhubarb from the garden and making a lip-smacking sauce for marinated pork belly, pan-fried until crispy in his hot and sour rhubarb crispy pork noodles, a special dish for any springtime occasion. He picks some new season spinach for a luxurious spinach and goats cheese risotto, before cooking up a scruffy spring tart using leeks. Jamie also demonstrates how to divide shop-bought basil into lots of individual plants to last through the year and celebrates the vibrantly coloured stalks and leaves of chard in a crowd-pleasing cheesy spring cannelloni

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp is enlisted to help identical twins Matt and Chris Wickham buy their first home in north London, but a budget of £350,000 may not be enough to secure a property in their favoured areas of Pimlico and Victoria. Meanwhile, Phil Spencer offers advice to Sam Taylor and his fiancee Krystle Maher, who are keen to move from their rented flat in Deptford into a place of their own

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with comedian Neil Delamere joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: A performance by childrens entertainer Nanny Gee in honour of Fredericks second birthday helps to shed some light on Frasiers romantic past, but leaves Lilith in a jealous rage. Comedy, guest starring Emma Thompson

programme.title: Cheers
programme.sub-title: A Diminished Rebecca with a Suspended Cliff
programme.desc: Rebecca claims to Sam that she can run the bar for a night just as efficiently as he, but has to reassess her boast when she discovers that she has forgotten to renew the liquor licence. Meanwhile, Woody adopts the role of cardsharp when a scorching game of poker develops in the poolroom

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Rebecca is left in charge of the bar while the others play poker but soon realises shes out of her depth when she discovers the liquor licence has expired. Bar-room comedy, starring Kirstie Alley

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Ed Jackson and Billy Monger present coverage of the opening events on day three of the Games in Italy. Menna Fitzpatrick, Hester Poole, Sam Cozens, Fred Warburton and Neil Simpson are among the British hopefuls in the super-G finals at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre. Plus, Great Britain take on South Korea in the round robin stage of the mixed team wheelchair curling at Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Ed Jackson and Billy Monger present coverage of the opening events on day three of the Games, as the super-G finals and South Korea v Great Britain in the round robin stage of the mixed team curling both continue at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre and Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium respectively

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Further live coverage of day three of the Games, including Italy v Great Britain in the round robin stage of the wheelchair curling mixed doubles at Cortina Olympic Curling Stadium. The British pairing of Jason Kean and Jo Butterfield are both making their Winter Paralympic debut, but Kean was part of the England team that qualified for the Wheelchair Curling World Championship in 2025, and Butterfield won a gold at the Summer Paralympics in the club throw back in 2016 in Rio

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Manchester, palliative care nurse and never a dull moment Pauline, is hoping to impress her guests with a family-style dinner party and plenty of home comforts. But Pauline kicks off her night by slipping up and forgetting to serve professional singer Danny, while cabaret practitioner Vanessa reveals an unexpected fear and jack-of-all-trades Richard is not impressed. Dental nurse Amy is intrigued by what Pauline does and her caring nature points the group to the answer. And a rousing choral performance that gets everyone involved delivers yet another side of Pauline. Will her snug soiree put her in the driving seat for the £1,000 prize?

programme.desc: Henry and Sam are in East Anglia with Nick, whos mad about Morris Minors. However, he also loves motorbikes, both British and Japanese, as well as vintage bicycles. Henry and Sam spot a few bikes they like, one in particular may need some serious restoration

programme.title: Shed and Buried
programme.desc: Henry and Sam are in Henrys home county of Oxfordshire in search of new bargains. They meet three brothers with containers full of bikes, plus many tractors, trucks and other trinkets. Sam is keen on getting a motorbike and Henry agrees, but Henry also has his eye on something else?

programme.title: Shed and Buried
programme.desc: Henry and Sam are back in Devon in search of new bargains. They meet Rodney at his workshop, full of signs, petrol pumps, vintage British cars and lots of rust! Rodney finds it hard to part with some items, but eventually a deal is struck..

programme.desc: Bowlero is a global bowling company with thousands of employees and hundreds of centres. It became a bowling giant almost overnight when it took over AMF and Bowlmor Lanes. Colie Edison created every element of the Bowlero brand. Now she wants to go undercover to find out how the new Bowlero is working for staff and customers. As her alter ego Michelle, who recently lost her job as a court reporter and now wants to find a second career, Colie works as a trainee manager, a lane server, an event host and a trainee facilities manager

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Adrian Perkins, the mayor of Shreveport, Louisiana, is in charge of 3,000 employees serving its 200,000 residents. Louisianas third largest city has fallen on hard times with crime and unemployment on the rise. Mayor Perkins wants to change the fortunes of his home town and he thinks going undercover will help. As his alter ego Wayne Oliver, an unemployed steel worker looking for a new career, he works alongside a firefighter, a police officer and a parks maintenance worker to see what life is like working for the city, and gets the chance to meet his community without them knowing that theyre talking to the mayor

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA

Gordon Ramsay heads to Easton, Pennsylvania, to help the troubled pair and save the business from financial disaster

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants he helped that were among the biggest challenges he faced, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he meets sisters Rita and Lisa to see if they have continued serving fresh instead of frozen food at Bostons La Galleria 33, before heading to the Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Massachusetts, to discover whether controlling owner Tom has kept his promise and handed over the reins to his daughter Andrea. Finally, Gordon goes back to the Prohibition gastro pub in Everett, Washington, to catch up with owner Rishi and learn if she has ditched belly-dancing in favour of running her business

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay uses his wealth of experience in the restaurant business to help struggling hotels, motels and BandBs across America. programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Adrenaline junkie groom-to-be Jai plans on having a wedding in the sky, neglecting the fact that his fiancee Leanne is terrified of heights

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Groom-to-be Chris decides to turn his wedding into the ultimate obstacle course, but his fiancee Charlotte dreams of a wedding day filled with family tradition

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Mitchell Modell, CEO of Modells Sporting Goods, goes incognito among his employees. He poses as a contestant on a fake reality TV show, and tries learn more about the people working for his company, which has been in his family for four generations since its founding by his great-grandfather in 1889

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Kat Cole goes undercover among the workforce of her company Cinnabon Incorporated, which serves sweet treats from more than 900 bakeries in 40 countries worldwide. Kat has come a long way after being raised by a single mother who fed her family of three girls on $10 a week. She seeks advice from her old boss, former Hooters CEO Coby Brooks, who also went undercover, in his own organisation. Kat has some lofty goals for Cinnabon to become a worldwide food brand, and wants to make sure that the new products she has put in place are working before she implements her plans

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Devon, married couple Suzanne and Gary kick off the competition with a menu featuring British classics with an Italian twist. Day two sees mega competitive Tony and Lisa cooking up some simple classics with one too many shop-bought ingredients. Finally its Nickie and Austens turn to impress with their sloppy but tasty grub. As the contest hots up, the hosts get a dose of their own medicine when their diners return the criticism theyve dished out all week

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Newcastle is the location as Rosalind and Roy present an evening of refined dining, Paul and Vanessa focus on their entertainment and Skev and Kayleigh serve up an eclectic menu

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Newcastle-upon-Tyne, ship workers Laura and her fiance Kevin launch the contest with their Scottish-themed menu. Competitive couple JP and Kayley have mixed feelings, and young professionals Jamie and Georgi think steak and chips is too basic. Jamie and Georgi host next and go all out with their fancy food - but are convinced JP and Kayley are out to sabotage their dishes. Finally, its the turn of cake shop owners Kayley and JP, who shock Jamie and Georgi with the standard of their food

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Hull, image-conscious Jay and Heather kick off the competition with a menu inspired by their love of American food. Day two sees thrifty home birds Mike and Stef host a family favourites evening, but will everyone appreciate their school dinner throwback? Finally its straight-talking Steve and Stephanies chance to show how fine dining should be done

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gordon Ramsay heads to Windsor, Vermont, on a mission to boost the fortunes of the Juniper Hill Inn, which may look impressive, but smells of raw sewage owing to a plumbing problem. After meeting the BandBs wealthy owner Robert Dean II, the chef uncovers some shocking treatment toward staff and realises he will need something approaching a miracle to save this business from disaster

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gordon Ramsay continues to uncover problems at the Juniper Hill Inn in Windsor, Vermont. The chef tries to help the staff communicate with owners Robert and Ari, but after he catches one of them telling a shocking lie, will it be the final straw?

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay checks in to the River Rock Inn in Milford, Pennsylvania, where owner Ken Pisciotta is struggling to attract guests and battling huge debts. He has all but given up and his constant negativity is rubbing off on the staff. Can Gordon get this establishment back on track before Ken loses everything?

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Supercar fanatic Eddie Kaen has spent millions on the design of the Keating in San Diego, California, but neglected to invest in things the guests really need. Gordon Ramsays visit uncovers empty rooms, a failing restaurant and staff at the end of their tether - leading to a shocking crisis that nobody saw coming

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay visits Meson de Mesilla, a Tuscan-style hotel in Las Cruces, New Mexico. Owner and former child-star Cali Szczawinski purchased the hotel so she could perform Cher songs every night to a captive audience, but Gordon investigates whether she may be falling flat when it comes to the needs of her guests because shes too busy trying to hit the high notes on stage

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants he helped that were among the biggest challenges he faced, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he meets sisters Rita and Lisa to see if they have continued serving fresh instead of frozen food at Bostons La Galleria 33, before heading to the Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Massachusetts, to discover whether controlling owner Tom has kept his promise and handed over the reins to his daughter Andrea. Finally, Gordon goes back to the Prohibition gastro pub in Everett, Washington, to catch up with owner Rishi and learn if she has ditched belly-dancing in favour of running her business

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling

programme.desc: Ina Garten puts a lunch cooked by her up for auction with the winning bidders testing chilled cucumber soup with shrimp, lobster and shells salad along with Eton Mess

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: Ina Garten shares her recipes for ricotta bruschetta, grilled steak and Arugula

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: Ina Garten provides a modern twist to classic recipes, including meatloaf and smoked salmon devilled eggs

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: Chef Ina Garten prepares a roasted butternut squash salad

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: Ina Garten prepares lemon chicken breasts and a recipe from one of her favourite New York restaurants

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: Recipes for desserts, including fruit filled cranberry and apple cake. There is also a masterclass from patisserie chef Claudia Fleming, who prepares lemon cream napoleon

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain focuses on something close to her heart - baking delicious treats to share with loved ones. Nadiya comes from a large family, so baking for others has always taken centre stage in her own home. She kicks things off with her chilli and cranberry brioche wreath, featuring fluffy indulgent dough balls surrounding an entire baked camembert cheese, creating the ultimate tear and share. Over the years, Nadiya has become an expert in catering for big family gatherings, and she has learnt that there is nothing better for impressing guests than a gigantic stack of delicious desserts. Her mini pecan pies filled with clotted cream ensure that everyone has their own little parcel of perfection. Next up is a cheats bread-and-butter pudding, using ice-cream and shop-bought croissants to slash the traditional cooking time without compromising on flavour

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: This time, Nadiya Hussain shares some of her favourite recipes for low-cost baking, using ingredients that do not break the bank or compromise on flavour. Dishes include fluffy onion pretzels, a potato rosti quiche, rhubarb and custard kisses, and her signature rich and indulgent chocolate fondant

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares some of her favourite recipes for biscuits and small bites to snack on, including chocolate-dipped florentines

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain presents four recipes for special occasions - an eight-layered birthday cake, a sparkly Mardi Gras king cake, jam-filled hot cross buns and a spectacularly festive biscuit tower

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: The chefs culinary tour of France leads him to the diverse and distinct region of the Camargue, a natural region located south of Arles. James begins at Les Marquises, the Laurent familys farm, where he learns about the Camargue bull, and barbecues local fish for the family. Later, he visits a rice farm and cooks a risotto using his own rice-brewed beer

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef arrives in the city of Arles, famous for its Roman architecture and the largest market in Provence. While there James sets up his mobile kitchen to cook a dish of John Dory with tomatoes, courgettes and clams - all from the stalls around him. He also has a go at pottery with a local ceramicist, visits an award-winning wine producer - who causes him to rethink the merits of rose wine - and heads to an olive grove where he cooks fried lamb chops with baby aubergines

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers cook comfort food dishes straight from the oven, including a new take on steak and chips and sticky gingerbread

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si and Dave turn up the heat on family favourites, including roasted spiced cauliflower cheese and a Caribbean-inspired plate of jerk pork chops

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Comfort Food

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prepare indulgent treats for when family and friends come on over - a baked cheesecake, a crowd pleasing terrine and the ultimate beef sandwich

programme.title: Man v Food

programme.title: Man v Food

programme.desc: In Syracuse, New York, Adam Richman takes on the Frittata Challenge - a breakfast dish weighing more than 6lb

programme.title: Man v Food

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Italy
programme.desc: Adams in Naples tasting iconic foods named after this city, including the classic Napolitina pizza and a pasta dish called spaghetti alle vongole

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef arrives in the riverside town of LIsle-sur-la-Sorgue in south-eastern France, where his culinary hero Keith Floyd once owned a restaurant. Buying produce from its floating market, and a Floyd-style hat, James sets up his mobile kitchen on a bridge overlooking the river, and cooks peaches pain perdu in the sunshine. Heading back in the direction of the town, James samples some truffle-laden dishes at renowned restaurant La Beaugraviere

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef heads to Normandy and sets up his mobile kitchen in one of the many calvados orchards in the area, making a dish of artichokes with hollandaise. He also visits restaurant Le Pave dAuge in Beuvron-en-Auge to meets a chef who likes to cook with as much butter as he does

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding and Matt Lucas present bread week, which kicks off with a sweet and savoury signature round. For the technical challenge, the bakers are asked to create a tribute to the NHS, while the showstopper is an ambitiously artistic challenge featuring decorative designs in bread. Judges Prue Leith and Paul Hollywood sample their efforts, and choose this weeks Star Baker - and this weeks departing contestant

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The competition starts to heat up during chocolate week, as Prue Leith and Paul Hollywood set three tricky challenges to test the remaining bakers. Theres a deceptively simple traybake in the signature, a technical packed full of chocolate and nuts, and a celebration showstopper that pushes the bakers skills with white chocolate to the limit. Who will impress Prue and Paul enough to keep their place in the tent, and who will be heading home? Presented by Matt Lucas and Noel Fielding

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Matt Lucas and Noel Fielding present the halfway point of the culinary contest, with pastry week. The bakers must put their own stamp on a Cornish classic and are set a retro technical challenge. For the showstopper round, they must concoct a classic tart in a pastry cage, before judges Prue Leith and Paul Hollywood decide who will be this weeks star baker and who will be going home

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding and Matt Lucas host the shows first ever Japanese week. For the signature challenge the bakers tackle a Japanese version of Asian staple steamed buns, while the technical round involves a tricky recipe involving multiple layers. The showstopper round is themed around kawaii - the Japanese love of all things cute

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Mississippi Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King explore the food and music of the American South, beginning with a trip to Memphis, Tennessee, where they try a soul stew and fried chicken

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Mississippi Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King continue their journey as they head to New Orleans, where they meet blues musician Little Freddie King and get to grips with some of the best local food, providing their own twist to a southern classic - po boy, a traditional sandwich

programme.desc: Jamie cooks an easy traybake, chicken and pepper jalfrezi. Then he shares a new way to use broccoli in stuffed pierogi dumplings

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Remaining contestants Holly, Janet, Jo and Mary-Anne test their pastry-making skills in the next set of challenges. Presented by Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The remaining three hopefuls compete in the 2011 final, which sees them cooking for a street party attended by judges Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood, as well as friends, family and former Bake Off contestants. Their offerings include mille-feuille, mini Victoria sponges, and miniature strawberry and rhubarb cheesecakes. Presented by Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins

programme.desc: Part one of two. The chefs culinary journey to discover the best buns and fabulous bakes in America continues with a visit to a Connecticut diner, where he enjoys a traditional pancake breakfast

programme.title: James Martins United Cakes of America

programme.title: James Martins United Cakes of America
programme.desc: The chef ventures into a hotel kitchen in Boston to learn why the citys cream pie is called the state dessert of Massachusetts, as his culinary journey to discover the best buns and most fabulous bakes in America continues

programme.desc: The chefs culinary journey to discover the best buns and most fabulous bakes in America continues with a visit to a Boston distillery, where he meets two brothers who are reviving the citys tradition for making the spirit before sampling a boozy rum cake

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef arrives in the riverside town of LIsle-sur-la-Sorgue in south-eastern France, where his culinary hero Keith Floyd once owned a restaurant. Buying produce from its floating market, and a Floyd-style hat, James sets up his mobile kitchen on a bridge overlooking the river, and cooks peaches pain perdu in the sunshine. Heading back in the direction of the town, James samples some truffle-laden dishes at renowned restaurant La Beaugraviere

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef heads to Normandy and sets up his mobile kitchen in one of the many calvados orchards in the area, making a dish of artichokes with hollandaise. He also visits restaurant Le Pave dAuge in Beuvron-en-Auge to meets a chef who likes to cook with as much butter as he does

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding and Matt Lucas present bread week, which kicks off with a sweet and savoury signature round. Judges Prue Leith and Paul Hollywood sample their efforts, and choose this weeks Star Baker - and this weeks departing contestant

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The competition starts to heat up during chocolate week, as Prue Leith and Paul Hollywood set three tricky challenges to test the remaining bakers. Who will impress Prue and Paul enough to keep their place in the tent, and who will be heading home? Presented by Matt Lucas and Noel Fielding

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Payment Day sees the BandBers take each other to task on everything from insufficient slumber to hopeless hosting, before the payments are revealed and we find out whos won

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the final night from Oxford and the turn of recruitment consultant and radio show host Ben to try and grab the grand. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Hosts Andrew and Diane are professional artists whove designed their establishment using all of their creative prowess, and the eclectic accommodation options are an immediate hit. programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: One guests pernickety protests about a path prove pivotal in providing a plaque-winner as the BandB owners reconvene for payment day

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: The latest batch of patients to be treated in the AandE department of Londons Kings College Hospital includes two with serious head injuries, including a cyclist from Brighton who has been airlifted to the building. programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: The stories of children and teenagers brought into AandE. Plus, a pregnant woman experiences intense pain following a sudden haemorrhage

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: On a Thursday day shift, a young man has come off his motorbike and slid along the road while not wearing any protective clothing. A young boy is complaining of chest pains; theres a family history of heart problems and his mum is worried about him. On the same shift, a man with only one leg has suspected deep vein thrombosis and needs to be treated in hospital. Paramedics arrive at an address to find a doctor in hospital scrubs holding a baby. Its a father and son and the baby has a worrying cough while a woman is complaining of severe abdominal pain

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A call comes in from the police saying a young man has collapsed outside a nightclub and isnt breathing. Its a category one emergency - the highest priority - meaning theres an immediate threat to life. A young woman is having a sickle cell crisis. This genetic condition makes her normally round blood cells form a sickle shape, sticking together and causing excruciating pain. A woman has the highest blood pressure that one of the paramedics has ever seen, and she needs to go to hospital urgently. A former soldier collapses and is trapped behind a door, and a woman has crippling chest pains. Her condition is a medical mystery that can only be solved with a trip to AandE

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: A selection of the teams favourite investigations. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off in rural Co Durham at Bowlees Farm Holiday Cottages, where married couple Tony and Viv Smith hope that their tranquil setting, private hot tubs and indoor swimming pool will impress their visitors. Their guests are bowled over by the views and the facilities, but friends Jane and Rachel find fault with the cleanliness in the kitchen. At dinner, its revealed that breakfast will be provided by the local farm shop, the only facility that Tony and Viv dont seem to have. Despite the clean plates, married couple Amanda and Howard make their feelings clear on the lack of options

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to the Piebald Inn in Hunmanby, North Yorkshire, where the lack of a thermostat and a divisive design idea irritate some of the guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed

At dinner, the guests are filled in about the Piebalds main attraction, but Jane and Viv dont see pie to pie about a design idea. The group members have a mixed nights sleep, but they all agree about the quality of the breakfast - which is topped off by a surprise from John

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The fourth and final venue is glamping site Evenlode Grounds in the Cotswolds, owned by Howard and Amanda Wooliams, who impress their guests by revealing it is also a working farm

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandB owners meet for the last time to find out what theyve been paid and to settle some scores. First up to fire back on their feedback are Tony and Viv, followed by John and Lesley, who are both desperate to know why Jane and Rachel were the only two who wouldnt return to their BandBs

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: The BMW 2002 Turbo was the first European production car to have a turbo-charged engine. It also happens to be on Tim Shaws all-time favourite list. The vehicle in question belongs to 79-year-old boy racer Raymond from the West Midlands, who has owned the car, which was built in 1974, for over 30 years. But Ray now has health problems and his beloved car has ended up rusting on his drive. When Tim and Fuzz Townshend get the vehicle to the workshop, they discover that its going to be even more work than they were expecting

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: On a Friday night, the paramedics are called out to a man who has reportedly collapsed in the street and isnt breathing. Paramedics also treat a man lying unconscious in an alleyway surrounded by needles whos taken a drug overdose but refuses to go to hospital, and a four-year-old who has a high temperature and a nasty cough. On the same shift, a man is struggling to breathe with an abnormal heart rhythm and a woman has fallen and cant get up. The paramedics need to make sure she hasnt broken any bones

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: The documentary following life inside an AandE department returns for its first series outside London - at the Queens Medical Centre, Nottingham. Sister Lou tells her story of working at the hospital for more than 30 years and how her life completely changed in her fifties. Forty-five-year-old Kirsty has been rushed into QMC after an accident at a fun run. The team members carry out extensive tests on 93-year-old John, who is suffering from shortness of breath, and in the childrens emergency unit, seven-year-old Hunter is being assessed after falling from a tree

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Sara, 46, is rushed in after a car crash with serious head and chest injuries, and 54 year-old Simon comes in after falling off his bike on his way to buy a helmet. As his condition deteriorates, Simon looks back on his life and the things he wishes he had done differently. Sixteen year-old James comes in with a severed finger after trying to climb a fence

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton heads to Kenya to see how cold-brew coffee differs from iced coffee. Helen Lawal tries frozen pastries in Portugal to see how well they perform well against fresh, while Briony May Williams heads to Staffordshire to learn how parsnips are cultivated to taste so sweet

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lee Juggurnauth heads to the west Mani Peninsula in Greece to help house-hunters Liz and Ian find their dream holiday home where they can enjoy the sunny climate and their love of outdoor sports. Lee lines up five impressive properties to show them for their budget of £280,000, but will any of them result in an offer?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps mother and son duo Dawn and Charlie find their dream holiday home around the town of Tavira on Portugals eastern Algarve with a budget of £180,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman heads to Famagusta in eastern Cyprus to help Mandy and Rae find the perfect property for their retirement. Now that their two daughters have grown up and have children of their own, theyre planning a permanent move to the Mediterranean island. Jasmine shows them the best of what their £150,000 budget can get them by sourcing five impressive properties for the pair to choose between

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at St Hilary Guest House in Llandudno, north Wales, where owner Tricia Watson and her daughter Jess hope that their classic seaside destination will impress. Guests Darren and Alex take a shine to their sink, but Debbie and Paul arent pleased with the plumbing. At dinner, theres food for thought when some of the guests culinary expertise is revealed, leaving the hosts in the hot seat. And theres a surprising revelation from Sue and Linda. At breakfast, cooking methods and quality are questioned, before a refusal to return has Tricia and Jess chewing over their feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second of this weeks destinations is The Falcon Retreat in south Wales, run by Sue Burston and her right-hand woman Linda Roberts. When the guests arrive at the secluded location, Darren and Alex are wowed by the view, but there are some issues in their room. At dinner, the guests are surprised by their hosts previous professions, and a conversation about the additional charge for breakfast worries Sue and Linda

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at the Crown Inn in Grewelthorpe, North Yorkshire, where owners Debbie Heartfield and Paul Dixon hope to impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final venue is Stix Glamping in Greetham, Rutland, where one guest decides glamping is not for her before she has even set foot on site, leaving her partner all alone

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Payment Day sees the BandBers take each other to task on everything from insufficient slumber to hopeless hosting, before the payments are revealed and we find out whos won

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Lyn kicks off the week by serving an intriguingly named soup. Will her guests competitive spirit knock Lyns confidence at her summer solstice shenanigans?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Kin hosts the second night in Oxford with hopes that his Asian-inspired menu and lucky red theme will win over his guests and ensure his victory. Jess struggles with her chopsticks and Alex cant stomach the soy pudding dessert but when Kin forgets to marinade his pork, everyone is left with dry meat

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Kitchen designer Jess plans a sophisticated menu on the third night in Oxford - but unfortunately, her soggy souffles do not go unnoticed by student Kin

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the fourth night in Oxford, fundraiser Monica sets out to take her guests on a culinary world tour adventure. Sadly, philosophy teacher Alex can no longer take part in the competition, so business radio DJ, Ben has stepped up to take his spot. However, from the moment Ben walks in and claims hes going to win the £1,000 prize, tensions rise. Kitchen designer Jess is blown away by Monicas food, but PhD student Kin is disappointed theres not more spice, but Ben certainly adds some when he accuses retired teacher Lyn of not being very interesting

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the final night from Oxford and the turn of recruitment consultant and radio show host Ben to try and grab the grand. With his British-Oxford-themed night set, PhD student Kin and Oxford graduate Monica come donned in their university gowns. Theres plenty of chaos in the kitchen, which sees the tuna overcooked, and Ben desperately trying to save his night with a sweet-and-fiery cottage pie

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Hosts Andrew and Diane are professional artists whove designed their establishment using all of their creative prowess, and the eclectic accommodation options are an immediate hit. But one guest thinks the facilities are lacking, and leaves some comments in their feedback that are difficult for the hosts to brush off

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mubi and Steve focus on fun and wellness at their place in Portsmouth, Ashbys Accommodation. The luxury lodgings go down brilliantly - until one guest happens across some maintenance mishaps. At least a trip up a famous tower seems to leave everyone in high spirits

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to Bosham BandB in West Sussex. Alicia runs the business from her own home, where she applies some extra homely touches for her guests comfort. Unfortunately, the relaxed mood is pierced during evening drinks by an argument about online reviews

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The weeks final visit is to Marc and Matts gastropub in Kent, The Dog at Wingham. The pair have raised the BandBers expectations throughout the week, and at first glance it seems they were right to do so. However, one visitor thinks theyve got a ruff deal with their room. The Dog is renowned for its food, so its hoped a perfect breakfast will put Marc and Matt onto a winner

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: One guests pernickety protests about a path prove pivotal in providing a plaque-winner as the BandB owners reconvene for payment day

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A 79-year-old man arrives in hospital after collapsing in the street whilst walking his dog, and doctors give him medication to try and stabilise his very high heart rate. Meanwhile, a young boy is treated after pushing a plastic toy down his ear, and doctors show concern for a man who has numbness in his arm and little finger, and send him for a CT scan

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: The latest batch of patients to be treated in the AandE department of Londons Kings College Hospital includes two with serious head injuries, including a cyclist from Brighton who has been airlifted to the building. Also brought in by helicopter is a father-of-four who fell from scaffolding as he repaired a roof, while other cases include former docker Bill, who has hurt his knee while drinking at his local working mens club

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: The stories of children and teenagers brought into AandE. Sixteen-year-old Ellie has suspected spinal injuries after being thrown from her horse and crushed against a fence, while consultant Jacqui Butler treats four-year-old Destiny, who has a fishbone stuck in his tonsils from his lunchtime curry. Plus, a pregnant woman experiences intense pain following a sudden haemorrhage

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: On a Thursday day shift, a young man has come off his motorbike and slid along the road while not wearing any protective clothing. A young boy is complaining of chest pains; theres a family history of heart problems and his mum is worried about him. On the same shift, a man with only one leg has suspected deep vein thrombosis and needs to be treated in hospital. Paramedics arrive at an address to find a doctor in hospital scrubs holding a baby. Its a father and son and the baby has a worrying cough while a woman is complaining of severe abdominal pain

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A call comes in from the police saying a young man has collapsed outside a nightclub and isnt breathing. Its a category one emergency - the highest priority - meaning theres an immediate threat to life. A young woman is having a sickle cell crisis. This genetic condition makes her normally round blood cells form a sickle shape, sticking together and causing excruciating pain. A woman has the highest blood pressure that one of the paramedics has ever seen, and she needs to go to hospital urgently. A former soldier collapses and is trapped behind a door, and a woman has crippling chest pains. Her condition is a medical mystery that can only be solved with a trip to AandE

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: The latest batch of patients to be treated in the AandE department of Londons Kings College Hospital includes two with serious head injuries, including a cyclist from Brighton who has been airlifted to the building. Also brought in by helicopter is a father-of-four who fell from scaffolding as he repaired a roof, while other cases include former docker Bill, who has hurt his knee while drinking at his local working mens club

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: The stories of children and teenagers brought into AandE. Sixteen-year-old Ellie has suspected spinal injuries after being thrown from her horse and crushed against a fence, while consultant Jacqui Butler treats four-year-old Destiny, who has a fishbone stuck in his tonsils from his lunchtime curry. Plus, a pregnant woman experiences intense pain following a sudden haemorrhage

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: A selection of the teams favourite investigations. Kate Quilton visits Haagen-Dazs to find out why some vanilla ice-creams are more expensive than others, while Helen Lawal heads to Tanzania to see the lengths vanilla farmers go to in the production of the natural flavouring. Matt Tebbutt travels to the Netherlands to explore the myth that Edam was once used as emergency cannon balls and Jimmy Doherty learns about an innovative solution to the problem of shellfish feeding on discarded plastic in the sea

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The competition begins in Stroud with horse trainer Melanie, who has opted for a quick and easy vegetarian menu so she can focus on a glamorous night of hosting - and partying

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Elliot hosts the second dinner party in Stroud and after making a good impression on radio presenter Kate, his guests are expecting big things. Tensions rise between Kate and fellow horse owner Mel as to who owns the most livestock leaving Belarusian rock fan Tasty and events manager Liv bored

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Belarusian rocker Tasty hosts the third night in Stroud and presents a taste of Mother Russia inspired by her culture and heritage. Kate and Mel squabble yet again as they come up with roles for their newly formed band, Elliots Angels, much to Livs disapproval. With some Russian dancing and pretend figure skating its an interesting night but will Tasty do enough to impress her guests

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the fourth evening in Stroud, Gloucestershire, marketing executive Liz incorporates her love of Italian food into her menu, hoping to impress her guests with large arancini balls, homemade ravioli and a rather lumpy panna cotta. Elliot feels his place at the top of the leaderboard is under threat, however Tatsy finds the constant chat about horses from Mel and Kate increasingly annoying

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final evening in Stroud, Gloucestershire, its a battle of the risottos as radio presenter Kate produces one very similar to that made earlier by arch-rival Mel. However, Kate has a secret weapon in the shape of a magical table decoration that may help her clinch the £1,000 prize

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton meets Andi and Neil from York, who dream of retiring permanently to Spain with their son Joel and spaniel Dixie. Theyre looking for a three-bedroom house with a dog-friendly garden, where barbeque enthusiast Neil can cook up a storm close to the beach close to the coastal town of Mazarron, near Murcia. Laura has five sizzling options for their budget of £145,000, but will any of them turn out to be their new forever home?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps Dawn and Ian, who want to fulfil their long-held dream of owning a house in Tavira, Portugal, for regular getaways with their two grown-up daughters and their dogs. Danni lines up five options for them to consider, all within their budget of £250,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lee Juggurnauth helps Harriet to find a holiday home in the popular resort of Villamartin on Spains Costa Blanca on a budget of £110,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Leah Charles-King is in south-west Cyprus helping house-hunter Uri find a permanent home for him and his wife Hazel. The pair, from Aberdeen, are keen to escape the cold Scottish winters anf have a budget of £200,000 to help realise their dreams

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lee Juggurnauth jets off to Portugals Algarve to help house-hunters Audrey and Tony find the perfect holiday home. Audrey and Tony first met five years ago while on holiday in the Algarve, so its always been a special place for them. Lee lines up five properties for them to consider, all within their budget of £220,000

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet the Farhalls in Windsor, who are unsure whether to sell their four-bedroom detached house or transform the layout of their home. Graham loves living in Windsor but Nardeth is growing tired of their cramped and dated house, and would prefer to look for a new property they could purchase

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp searches across Balham and Putney in south-west London, with Hannah and Ben, who are ready to buy after years of renting. They have a good budget, but first-time buyers nerves have led to 40-odd viewings and no deal. Phil Spencer helps Hemani and Nigel, whos from Australia, as they seek an Aussie-proportioned property in the Thames Valley

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: This week kicks off at Naim and Saras Lovelady Shield Hotel in Cumbria. The views are welcomed by most of the Bandguests - but one pair are preoccupied by the furniture

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The weeks second visit comes from the Ryebeck Hotel in the Lake District, with glorious views of Lake Windermere. But its other views left in the feedback that take centre stage

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandBers go off-grid at Dale2Swale Glamping in Richmond, North Yorkshire, but some guests are worried about how theyll survive the night without their creature comforts

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week takes the BandBers to Ashbourne Holidays in Derbyshire, where deer wander around the grounds and the guests pay a visit to a local animal park

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: On payment day, the BandBers meet for the last time, and clash over furniture failings and clock complaints. Sparks fly - rather ironically - over a lack of electricity

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics treat a man whos having a heart attack but thinks hes just pulled a muscle. He has no history of cardiac issues, but an examination confirms that his heart is blocked. A day out hiking ends badly for a man when he falls and injures his ankle, and the paramedics tea and toast making skills are needed for a diabetic patient.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: The stories of people who changed the course of their lives by taking huge risks, including a consultant who swapped an army career for the frontline of emergency medicine. programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics find a woman whos fallen, is on blood thinners and may have a bleed on the brain.
